title: Kalshi Temperature Event
description: A daily temperature event for one station. Contains multiple markets (brackets). E.g., KXHIGHJFK-26APR25 = 'What will the high temperature at JFK be on April 25, 2026?' Typically 6 markets per event (4 middle brackets of 2°F, 2 open-ended edge brackets).

station_code.description: NWS station code for this event.

event_date.description: The observation date this event covers (YYYY-MM-DD). Settlement uses CLI for this date.

measure_type.description: Temperature measure: daily high or daily low.

status.description: Event lifecycle status. Open = trading active. Closed = trading ended, awaiting settlement. Settled = CLI published, all markets resolved.

market_count.description: Number of markets (brackets) in this event. Typically 6.

open_time.description: When the event opened for trading (RFC3339). Typically 10 AM ET the prior day.

close_time.description: When trading closed (RFC3339). Typically close of trading day.

settlement_time.description: When the event was settled (RFC3339). 10:00 AM ET the day after event_date. Null if not yet settled.

settlement_value.description: The CLI temperature that settled this event (°F). From NWS Daily Climate Report. Null if not yet settled.

$comment: BUSINESS RULES: (1) settlement_time is 10:00 AM ET the day after event_date. (2) settlement_value comes from NWS CLI high_temp_f or low_temp_f depending on measure_type. (3) An event is 'stale' if status is still 'open' more than 36 hours after event_date. (4) All markets in an event must have the same series_ticker and event_date. (5) market_count is typically 6 (4 middle + 2 edge brackets) but can vary.
